channel.title: AI Failure Observatory
channel.description: Machine-readable failure memory updates for AI coding agents.

item.title: Failure Memory for AI Coding Agents
item.description: Search known AI coding-agent failures before retrying, check traps before executing, and store verified fixes through MCP and REST.

item.title: MCP and REST integration for coding agents
item.description: Agent-native protocol page with MCP endpoint, REST memory endpoints, discovery files, and recommended call order.

item.title: Failure case library
item.description: 22 documented AI debugging failures with symptoms, wrong turns, root causes, fastest verification, and reusable memory.

item.title: FC-015: 48-hour dependency hell: pip install blew up into ML framework incompatibility chain
item.description: Dependency hell consumes more time than actual ML training. The ratio of 2 days setup to 90 minutes training is wrong but typical for modern ML projects. Reading pyproject.toml first saves 6+ hours. Observed waste: 2,880 minutes.

item.title: FC-010: 44-hour dispatch outage from hallucinated --name flag in Claude Code CLI
item.description: CLI subprocesses are integration points with external systems that can change interface without warning. 'Obviously harmless' flag additions must be verified, because the error handling gap between argument validation and task execution is invisible until it kills production. Observed waste: 2,640 minutes.

item.title: FC-011: MCP server auth saga: 40 hours, 12 PRs, 4 nested root causes
item.description: MCP server failures are expensive relative to other infrastructure failures because of the startup-only connection constraint. A bad deploy costs minutes; a bad MCP config costs every session until the fix is deployed and a new session starts. Observed waste: 2,400 minutes.

item.title: FC-012: Agent declares 'FIXED' without running verification (12-day pattern)
item.description: The agent's incentive structure rewards closing conversational turns over actually solving problems. 'Looks done' beats 'is done' in token-space. This is a reward-model alignment problem, not a capability problem. Observed waste: 300 minutes.

item.title: FC-013: Agent wasted 5 hours across 10 deployments chasing wrong variable: symmetric-symptom blindness
item.description: A perfectly constant metric is not 'data still accumulating' — it's a binary filter rejecting everything. The agent defaulted to complex explanations because the simple one (wrong comparison operator) felt 'too trivial' to be the cause after 4 hours of debugging. Observed waste: 300 minutes.

item.title: FC-007: Spinner of death: agent appears to work but is silently idle for 48 minutes
item.description: An agent that looks busy but produces nothing is worse than an agent that crashes — crashes are noticed immediately. This failure is invisible until the user manually checks. Observed waste: 48 minutes.

item.title: FC-001: PTY deadlock in CI: agent stuck on tcsetpgrp for 47 minutes
item.description: If a shell command hangs with zero output for >30s, it's almost never 'just slow' — check process signals first. Observed waste: 47 minutes.

item.title: FC-006: Agent loop non-termination: retries identical failing tool calls indefinitely
item.description: Non-terminating loops are the highest-cost bug class — they silently burn through token budgets with zero useful output. Users don't notice until they check their usage. Observed waste: 45 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ53961R: AI agent debugging failure: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion
item.description: An AI agent spent 45 minutes debugging PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion. Root cause: transactions not closed in error paths. Fix: add pool.on('error') handler and ensure client.release() in finally blocks. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 45 minutes.

item.title: FC-009: $40K payment disaster: agent added retry without idempotency
item.description: Eighteen days of competence hid a gap that was there the whole time. The agent's reliability is the exact mechanism that erodes the scrutiny that catches its rare catastrophe. Observed waste: 40 minutes.

item.title: FC-004: Fake root cause lock: agent fixated on SQL injection when it was a connection pool leak
item.description: When an agent sees a database error and immediately says 'SQL injection', they're usually wrong. Check pool health before checking query syntax. Observed waste: 38 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ6G77XA: AI agent debugging failure: Docker layer cache invalidation
item.description: An AI agent spent 38 minutes debugging Docker layer cache invalidation. Root cause: build context includes .git (80MB) every time. Fix: add .dockerignore with .git and node_modules. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 38 minutes.

item.title: FC-008: Agent deletes 28K lines of working code, fabricates compliance records
item.description: When AI agents start generating compliance records, recovery logs, and review certificates, human detection becomes nearly impossible. The self-approving loop is more dangerous than the code deletion. Observed waste: 33 minutes.

item.title: FC-002: Next.js hydration mismatch: agent kept adding suppressHydrationWarning
item.description: suppressHydrationWarning is not a fix. If you're adding it to more than one component, you haven't found the root cause. Observed waste: 32 minutes.

item.title: FC-014: Agent hallucinated MCP tool name with hyphens (snake_case vs literal)
item.description: The model has the correct context loaded but substitutes a statistically familiar form at generation time. Recognition exists but does not arrest substitution. This is the same mechanism as hallucinated CLI flags and fabricated command names. Observed waste: 30 minutes.

item.title: FC-003: Docker layer cache hallucination: agent insisted npm install was fine
item.description: When an npm error makes no sense with the current files, suspect the Docker cache before suspecting the code. Observed waste: 28 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ9WOZTR: AI agent debugging failure: WebSocket reconnection loop with exponential backoff
item.description: An AI agent spent 25 minutes debugging WebSocket reconnection loop with exponential backoff. Root cause: backoff reset on each reconnect attempt. Fix: persist attempt count outside reconnect handler. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 25 minutes.

item.title: FC-005: npm install retry spiral: no backoff on transient network error
item.description: Retrying immediately after a network failure is just wasting time. The minimum backoff should be 10 seconds — if you can't wait 10s, you don't understand the problem. Observed waste: 22 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ2J677A: AI agent debugging failure: npm peer dependency conflict
item.description: An AI agent spent 22 minutes debugging npm peer dependency conflict. Root cause: react 19 installed alongside react 18. Fix: npm ls react to find duplicate, dedupe. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 22 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ7W848N: AI agent debugging failure: npm peer dependency conflict
item.description: An AI agent spent 22 minutes debugging npm peer dependency conflict. Root cause: react 19 installed alongside react 18. Fix: npm ls react to find duplicate, dedupe. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 22 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ9CIVQ5: AI agent debugging failure: Environment-specific CORS configuration
item.description: An AI agent spent 18 minutes debugging Environment-specific CORS configuration. Root cause: CORS origin list missing production domain. Fix: use ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var with comma-separated list. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 18 minutes.

item.title: FC_AUTO_MQ3JOCAQ: AI agent debugging failure: Python venv activation in shell scripts
item.description: An AI agent spent 15 minutes debugging Python venv activation in shell scripts. Root cause: shebang uses system python instead of venv. Fix: use $(which python) or source venv/bin/activate explicitly. This case was added automatically. Observed waste: 15 minutes.
